First, I need to understand what $ 17_b $ and $ 97_b $ represent in base $ b $. In any base $ b $, the digits must be less than the base. So, since the digits here go up to 9 (in $ 97_b $), the base must be greater than 9, which is already given. So that's good. Let me convert both numbers from base $ b $ to decimal (base 10) to work with them more easily. Starting with $ 17_b $. In base $ b $, this number is equal to $ 1 \times b^1 + 7 \times b^0 = b + 7 $. Similarly, $ 97_b $ in decimal is $ 9 \times b^1 + 7 \times b^0 = 9b + 7 $. So the problem is asking for bases $ b > 9 $ such that $ b + 7 $ divides $ 9b + 7 $.
